ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn term exists for Rougamo (Chinese meat-filled flatbread). 'Sandwich food product' (FOODON_03315671) is used as the closest parent category representing a bread-enclosed meat filling.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP (meat-filled bread product) used as closest parent. No specific Rougamo entry exists in FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for Rougamo or Chinese meat-filled flatbread as a specific food concept.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving of Spicy Chicken Rougamo is approximately 200 g, consisting of one baked flatbread (mo) filled with a generous portion of braised spicy chicken.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken sandwich / stuffed flatbread composite — estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for braised chicken meat (NDB 05064), enriched wheat flatbread (NDB 18023), and chilli-garlic seasoning components
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for Spicy Chicken Rougamo exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, FAO/INFOODS SEA, or USDA FoodData Central. Values are estimated by component-based calculation: approximately 55% baked wheat flatbread (mo), 40% braised spiced chicken, and 5% chilli-garlic-oil seasoning, weighted per 100 g of assembled product. USDA SR Legacy entries used as primary ingredient anchors. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of wheat flatbread and mixed meat-bread products — analogous estimate based on white wheat bread GI reference (GI 75) and braised meat contribution reducing overall GI; closest analogous food: pita bread with meat filling (GI ~55)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Spicy Chicken Rougamo. GI estimated at 55 (low, at the upper bound of the low category) based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for pita bread with meat filling and mixed wheat-bread-meat products. The baked wheat flatbread (mo) is the primary glycemic driver; the braised chicken and fat content moderate the glycemic response. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 57.0) / 100 = 31.4, where carbohydrate per 200 g serving = 57.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~520 mg per 100 g; ~1040 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit Spicy Chicken Rougamo to occasional consumption, as one serving provides approximately 1040 mg sodium — nearly 45% of the recommended daily limit of 2300 mg — and the chilli-soy braising sauce contributes substantially to this sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~55, at the upper bound of the low category) and moderate glycemic load (~31 per serving) from wheat flatbread
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume Spicy Chicken Rougamo in moderation; the wheat flatbread contributes a low GI of approximately 55 (at the upper bound of the low category) and a glycemic load of ~31 per serving, which may cause a moderate postprandial glucose rise, and portion control is advised alongside pairing with low-GI vegetables.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (~2.1 g per 100 g) and high sodium (~520 mg per 100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, Spicy Chicken Rougamo poses a moderate concern due to its combined sodium and saturated fat content; choosing lean chicken breast, reducing soy sauce in preparation, and limiting serving frequency can help manage cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Spicy Chicken Rougamo (辣鸡肉夹馍, Là Jī Ròu Jiā Mó) is a Chinese street food originating from Shaanxi province, China. It consists of a baked wheat flatbread known as 'mo' (馍) — a dense, slightly crispy unleavened or leavened round bread — split open and stuffed with braised or stewed spiced chicken. The chicken filling is seasoned with chilli, garlic, ginger, soy sauce, Sichuan peppercorn, and spring onion, giving it a bold, spicy, and savoury flavour profile with a tender, juicy texture contrasting the chewy flatbread exterior. Rougamo is often described as the 'Chinese hamburger' and is one of China's oldest street foods, with origins dating back over 2,000 years. The spicy chicken variant is a modern adaptation of the traditional pork rougamo, made popular in Singapore and Southeast Asia through Chinese noodle and street food restaurants. Nutritionally, one 200 g serving provides approximately 496 kcal, 29 g protein, 16 g fat, and 57 g carbohydrate. The sodium content is high (~1040 mg per serving), making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The low glycemic index (~55, at the upper bound of the low category) and moderate glycemic load (~31) require portion awareness for people with Type 2 Diabetes. The dish is generally halal when prepared with chicken and halal-certified soy sauce, making it accessible to Muslim consumers in Singapore.
common_user_queries: What is rou jia mo made of?

confidence_reason: No direct entry for Spicy Chicken Rougamo exists in any primary approved source (HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are estimated by component-based calculation using USDA SR Legacy ingredient anchors. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using pita bread with meat filling as the closest comparable food. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: Nutrient values are component-estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (braised chicken, wheat flatbread, chilli-garlic seasoning) as no direct approved-source entry exists for this dish. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy component entries used for this estimation. All other -1 fields are not reported in the source database for the component ingredients used. GI of 55 (low category) is an analogous estimate based on the University of Sydney GI Database pita-with-meat-filling reference; direct GI measurement for Spicy Chicken Rougamo has not been published. Sodium is notably high due to soy sauce and spiced braising liquid in the chicken filling; actual sodium may vary by restaurant preparation. The dish is classified as halal-suitable when prepared with halal-certified chicken and soy sauce, as is standard at the source restaurant (Lan Ting Xu Lanzhou Beef Noodles). Serving size of 200 g is an estimate for one standard rougamo sandwich as served in Singapore food delivery context.
